easy.title: Viewport Comparison Demo
easy.description: Create a page that demonstrates the difference between having and not having a viewport meta tag. Show two iframes side-by-side: one with viewport tag and one without. Add CSS to make the difference visually explainable with annotations.
easy.starterCode: With vs. Without Viewport
easy.solution:
With vs. Without Viewport Meta Tag

❌ Without Viewport

✅ With Viewport

medium.title: Safe Area Implementation
medium.description: Create a page that demonstrates safe area insets for notched devices. Build a mobile-app style layout with a top status bar, scrollable content, and a bottom tab bar. Use viewport-fit=cover and env(safe-area-inset-*) to properly pad content away from notches and rounded corners.
medium.starterCode: <!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head><meta charset="UTF-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover"><title>Safe Area Demo</title><style>/* Add safe area padding */</style></head><body><!-- Mobile app layout --></body></html>
medium.solution:
My App

This page uses viewport-fit=cover with env(safe-area-inset-*) for safe area padding.

🔵 Safe area inset top: env(safe-area-inset-top)
🔵 Safe area inset bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom)

The header adds safe-area-inset-top padding to prevent content from rendering behind the device notch. The bottom tab bar adds safe-area-inset-bottom padding for the home indicator area.

Note: If you're not on a notched device, fallback values (20px, 0px, 10px) are used instead.

🏠 Home
🔍 Search
❤️ Favorites
👤 Profile

hard.title: Responsive Canvas with Dynamic Viewport Units and Virtual Keyboard Handling
hard.description: Build a chat-style interface that uses dynamic viewport units (dvh) to properly handle the virtual keyboard. Include a header, scrollable message area, and fixed input bar. On mobile, when the keyboard opens, the input bar should stay above it using dvh units.
hard.starterCode: <!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head><meta charset="UTF-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"><title>Chat with dvh</title><style>/* Use dvh for keyboard handling */</style></head><body><!-- Chat layout --></body></html>
hard.solution:
Chat with dvh
Hey! How are you?
I'm great, thanks! Using dvh units for this chat.
What's dvh?
Dynamic Viewport Height - it adapts when the keyboard opens on mobile!
